drop2

FAQ

What are the size limits?

Browser sends without an account are limited to 10 MiB per file. The CLI supports larger files, folders, and live streaming — install with curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xreal/drop2/main/scripts/install.sh | bash.

How long does a link stay available?

You choose when creating a share: delete after the first download, or keep it for up to one week in the browser. Stored shares expire automatically and are removed from storage.

Who can read my files?

Only someone with the share link and PIN. Files are encrypted on your device with modern cryptography (X25519 + XChaCha20-Poly1305). drop2 infrastructure stores ciphertext only and cannot decrypt your content.

What about misuse or abuse?

drop2 is for legitimate file sharing, not hosting public content. Repeated wrong PIN attempts trigger cooldowns; cross-share probing is rate-limited. We do not inspect file contents, but shares can be removed when they violate acceptable use.

Is the PIN enough protection?

The PIN is an access gate on top of encryption — send it separately from the link. For highly sensitive data, treat the link like a secret and prefer short expiry or delete-after-download.

Send GitHub
